title: Save Plays: Downgrades, Pauses, and Recovery Calls

questions.question: What percentage of paused customers typically reactivate, compared to cancelled customers?

options.text: They reactivate at the same rate

questions.explanation: 60-70% of paused customers reactivate vs. only 5-15% of cancelled customers. A pause keeps data intact and the door open, making reactivation dramatically easier than winning back a fully churned customer.

questions.question: What is the core principle behind all save plays?

options.text: Give the customer a large discount to stay
A customer paying $50/month is infinitely more valuable than a churned customer paying $0
All customers who want to cancel should be let go immediately
Save plays only work for enterprise customers

questions.explanation: The core principle: a customer paying any amount is infinitely more valuable than a churned customer paying $0. Every save play — whether downgrade, pause, or recovery call — beats losing the customer entirely.

questions.question: When should you offer a downgrade instead of a pause?

options.text: When the customer's need has decreased but not disappeared
When the customer has been with you less than 3 months

questions.explanation: A downgrade makes sense when the customer's need has decreased but not disappeared: they're using fewer features, their team shrank, business volume dropped, or they have budget constraints but still see value.

questions.question: Recovery calls for high-value accounts ($200+/month) can save 30-50% of at-risk accounts when conducted within 24 hours.
questions.explanation: True. Personal recovery calls within 24 hours save 30-50% of high-value at-risk accounts. The key is speed and genuine curiosity about what went wrong, combined with a concrete save offer (downgrade, pause, or problem resolution).

questions.question: Design your save play playbook with specific scripts for each scenario: a customer who wants to cancel due to budget, one who says they're not using the product enough, and one who's switching to a competitor. What would you offer in each case?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response provides three distinct save play scripts tailored to different cancellation reasons, includes specific offers (downgrade tier/pricing, pause duration, recovery call structure), and demonstrates empathy rather than desperation in the communication approach.
